heading: Reporting of certain professional review actions taken by health care entities
label: 11133 Reporting of certain professional review actions taken by health care entities

children.heading: Reporting by health care entities
children.label: a Reporting by health care entities
A
children.text: takes a professional review action that adversely affects the clinical privileges of a physician for a period longer than 30 days;

children.label: i
children.text: while the physician is under an investigation by the entity relating to possible incompetence or improper professional conduct, or

children.label: ii
children.text: in return for not conducting such an investigation or proceeding; or

children.label: C
children.text: in the case of such an entity which is a professional society, takes a professional review action which adversely affects the membership of a physician in the society,

children.heading: Permissive reporting on other licensed health care practitioners
children.label: 2 Permissive reporting on other licensed health care practitioners
children.text: A health care entity may report to the Board of Medical Examiners, in accordance with , the information described in paragraph (3) in the case of a licensed health care practitioner who is not a physician, if the entity would be required to report such information under paragraph (1) with respect to the practitioner if the practitioner were a physician.section 11134(a) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: the name of the physician or practitioner involved,

children.label: B
children.text: a description of the acts or omissions or other reasons for the action or, if known, for the surrender, and

children.label: C
children.text: such other information respecting the circumstances of the action or surrender as the Secretary deems appropriate.

children.heading: Reporting by Board of Medical Examiners
children.label: b Reporting by Board of Medical Examiners
children.text: Each Board of Medical Examiners shall report, in accordance with , the information reported to it under subsection (a) and known instances of a health care entity’s failure to report information under subsection (a)(1).section 11134 of this title

children.heading: Health care entities
children.label: 1 Health care entities
children.text: A health care entity that fails substantially to meet the requirement of subsection (a)(1) shall lose the protections of if the Secretary publishes the name of the entity under .section 11111(a)(1) of this titlesection 11111(b) of this title

children.heading: Board of Medical Examiners
children.label: 2 Board of Medical Examiners
children.text: If, after notice of noncompliance and providing an opportunity to correct noncompliance, the Secretary determines that a Board of Medical Examiners has failed to report information in accordance with subsection (b), the Secretary shall designate another qualified entity for the reporting of information under subsection (b).

children.heading: References to Board of Medical Examiners
children.label: d References to Board of Medical Examiners
children.text: Any reference in this subchapter to a Board of Medical Examiners includes, in the case of a Board in a State that fails to meet the reporting requirements of or subsection (b), a reference to such other qualified entity as the Secretary designates.section 11132(a) of this title
